info.description: Headless Fleet API. Agents, integrations, connections, triggers, threads, runs.
info.title: Fleet API

get.description: Returns integrations available to the workspace.

post.description: Registers a new custom integration. Type is forced to CUSTOM.

delete.description: Idempotent. Returns 204 whether or not the integration existed.

patch.description: Updates mutable fields. URL is immutable.

put.description: Replaces the integration's full list of supported auth methods.

get.description: Returns tools from a remote MCP server. Serves cached results when fresh, otherwise fetches from the remote server and caches the response.

parameters.description: MCP server URL to fetch tools from
parameters.name: url

parameters.description: OAuth provider ID for token-based auth
parameters.name: oauth_provider_id

parameters.description: Skip cache and fetch fresh tools
parameters.name: force_refresh

get.description: Returns MCP servers visible to the caller after ABAC filtering. Service-key callers may pass X-Ls-User-Id to resolve per-user OAuth providers.

parameters.description: User UUID for per-user OAuth resolution (service keys)
parameters.name: X-Ls-User-Id

post.description: Registers a new MCP server configuration for the workspace.

get.description: Returns a single MCP server by ID.

patch.description: Partially updates an MCP server. Tool list cache is invalidated on success.

post.description: Discovers and registers an OAuth provider for a user against an MCP server configured with per-user dynamic client mode. Idempotent when a mapping already exists.
Generate a GitHub OAuth link for the current user to connect their GitHub account.

get.description: Return the current user's GitHub connection status and metadata.

delete.description: Remove the current user's GitHub connection and all linked installations.

patch.description: Update the actor preference for the current user's GitHub connection.

post.description: Generate a link to install the GitHub App for the current organization.

get.description:
Return GitHub App installations linked to the current user from
our database. This is a cache — it does not hit the GitHub API.
Use POST /installations/refresh to force a fresh sync from GitHub.

post.description:
Trigger a live sync with GitHub for the current user's GitHub App
installations. Upserts new installations, deletes stale ones, and
returns the refreshed list. Requires an existing GitHub connection.

delete.description: Remove a GitHub App installation link for the current user.

get.description: Return repositories accessible to the specified GitHub App installation.
get.summary: List repositories for a GitHub App installation

post.description: Return a short-lived GitHub access token scoped to the given repository.

Process GitHub App webhooks (installation lifecycle, installation_repositories). GitHub may deliver the same event more than once; handling re-applies the current repository-selection state and is safe to repeat.

get.description: Returns the top agents in the workspace with cost data for the given time window. Defaults to sorting by cost descending; pass sort_by and sort_order to change. Data is sourced entirely from ClickHouse run metadata.
Returns the top models used in the workspace with aggregated cost and distinct run counts for the given time window. Defaults to sorting by cost descending; pass sort_by and sort_order to change.
Returns the top tools in the workspace with run counts, latency, and agent counts for the given time window. Defaults to sorting by runs descending; pass sort_by and sort_order to change.
Returns the top users with aggregated cost data for the given time window. Defaults to sorting by cost descending; pass sort_by and sort_order to change.

parameters.description: Sort field (cost, runs, agent_count, last_used, first_used)
parameters.name: sort_by
